content.full_text:
THE PRIMACY GROUP

3609 FOURTH AVE.
SAN DIEGO
CALIFORNIA 92103
(619) 295-6923
FAX (619) 295-0487

Luisa Menchaca
Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC)
428 J Street #600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

The purpose of this letter is to confirm our conversation of last week. I am writing to give us each a copy for our files.

I am a political consultant and one of my clients is running for County Board of Supervisors in San Diego.

Another of my clients is one of the employee organizations at the County of San Diego, the Deputy Sheriffs' Assn. (DSA). I am representing them in connection with a local initiative measure, Proposition C, that is on the November ballot.

Incidentally, the DSA is supporting my client for Supervisor. I do not represent the DSA with regard to their activities on behalf of my client and they are scrupulous about not informing me in advance of any independent activities they undertake on behalf of my client.

The DSA -- and several other law enforcement organizations -- have asked me to produce a mailer on their behalf in San Diego County expressing their support for nearly a dozen candidates and ballot measures (including my client).

It is their intention that this mailer be done as an independent expenditure and not be coordinated with any candidate or campaign. Though the mailer will be artistically designed by one of my staff, they are determining its content and to whom it is being mailed. I am simply providing them vendor services for this mailer.

I expressed concern to you on the phone that I wanted to make certain that this arrangement did not violate any prohibitions regarding cooperation between independent campaigns under the law. You stated clearly to me, according to my notes, that this arrangement, as I described it, posed "no problem with the Fair Political Practices Act."

Thank you for your assistance.

Yours truly,

Larry Remer
